stories.headline: Trump says he will push for peace in Ukraine after meeting Zelenskyy at G7
stories.summary: US president says he will do what he can to end the Russia-Ukraine war after a 'very good' meeting in France.

stories.headline: Kyiv seeks license to produce Patriot systems for Ukraine and Europe
stories.summary: With Russia deliberately targeting Ukraine’s energy system each winter, Volodymyr Zelenskyy said at the G7 summit that he wants to bring the war to an end before the next cold season. Kyiv now holds a stronger position both on the battlefield and in its strikes deep in Russia.

stories.headline: FBI thwarted plot targeting White House UFC event, Patel says
stories.summary: US media say the plan would have involved a drone attack. The FBI boss says multiple suspects are in custody.

stories.headline: Iranian football team told to return to Mexico after opening World Cup game
stories.summary: Head coach of Tehran’s squad says ‘oppressed’ players instructed to go back to training base over border

stories.headline: Qatar’s Emir hails Iran deal and touts US investments in Trump meeting
stories.summary: Qatar’s Emir hails Iran deal and touts US investments in Trump meeting.

stories.headline: Russian artist and critic of Putin and Chechen leader shot dead in ⁠Poland
stories.summary: Two Belarusians ⁠detained over attack on Robert ⁠Kuzovkov, who is also known as Semyon SkrepetskyA Russian artist critical of Vladimir Putin and the Chechen leader, Ramzan Kadyrov, has been shot and killed in ⁠the eastern Polish town of Biała Podlaska, a prosecutor has said.Five shots were fired at the ⁠victim, including one ⁠to the head, in the attack on Monday, ​said Marcin Kozak, a spokesperson for the district prosecutor in Lublin. stories.headline: Iran says Israeli occupation in Lebanon would breach US deal
stories.summary: Iran says any continued Israeli occupation in Lebanon would be a breach of the Memorandum of Understanding with the US.

stories.headline: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
stories.summary: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.headline: Oil sinks below $80 a barrel as traders bet Strait of Hormuz flows will return
stories.summary: Brent crude has tumbled to three-month low following agreement to extend US-Iran ceasefire

stories.headline: Oil Falls Below $80 With US-Iran Deal Set to Add Wave of Supply
stories.summary: Brent oil fell below $80 a barrel for the first time in more than three months as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts and regional benchmarks collapsing.

stories.headline: Germany Rejects UniCredit’s Commerzbank Bid on Last Day
stories.summary: Germany renewed its staunch opposition to UniCredit SpA’s proposed €39 billion ($45 billion) takeover of Commerzbank AG on the final day of the formal acceptance period.

stories.headline: FBI thwarted attack targeting UFC event at White House, director says
stories.summary: People have been taken into custody in connection with the alleged planned attack on the UFC event Sunday that President Donald Trump attended, FBI Director Kash Patel said.

stories.headline: Fujitsu chair resigns after ‘woman-related inappropriate conduct’
stories.summary: Japanese technology company at centre of Post Office IT scandal is negotiating settlement with UK government over faulty softwareBusiness live – latest updatesThe chair of Fujitsu, the Japanese technology firm at the centre of the Post Office IT scandal, has resigned after its board became aware of his “woman-related inappropriate conduct”.The company said on Tuesday that Hidenori Furuta had stepped down after two years in the role. stories.summary: Thinktank says decoupling electricity from gas prices has also helped shield Spain from hikes caused by Iran warSpanish households save €10 a month on electricity bills because of wind turbines and solar panels installed in the last five years, a report has found.Typical energy bills would be 19% more expensive if electricity costs were still as tightly coupled to gas prices as in 2021, according to Ember, a climate thinktank. stories.headline: Red Cross warns Ebola outbreak in DRC not yet peaked, could last a year
stories.summary: The Red Cross says it is 'very difficult' to know the extent at which the epidemic is spreading.

stories.headline: Iran fans react to team’s first World Cup match amid political schisms
stories.summary: Iran fans react to team’s first World Cup match amid political schisms

stories.headline: Football fans celebrate the World Cup across continents and cultures
stories.summary: Fans in jerseys and painted faces gather to watch World Cup matches, celebrating unity and rivalry alike.

stories.headline: Russian artist and Putin critic Semyon Skrepetsky shot dead in Poland, officials say
stories.summary: Skrepetsky was known for his sometimes provocative caricatures, which targeted prominent Russian political figures ranging from Putin and Soviet leader Joseph Stalin.

stories.headline: MEPs approve EU-US trade deal despite Trump’s new trade war threats
stories.summary: The agreement removes EU tariffs on US goods while accepting a 15 percent levy in the other direction.

stories.headline: Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
stories.summary: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

stories.headline: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam paper leak concerns
stories.summary: The NEET exam had earlier been scrapped over allegations of a paper leak, sparking massive protests.

stories.headline: ECB’s Escriva Says Energy Disruption Will Persist Despite Deal
stories.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Jose Luis Escriva warned that complications over energy supplies are likely to persist, despite an agreement to re-open the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: What to Watch in Primary and Runoff Elections in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
stories.summary: The top race of the day is in Georgia, where Republican voters will pick a nominee to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff, a Democrat.

stories.headline: RBA interest rates: Reserve Bank holds official cash rate at 4.35%
stories.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia decision on Tuesday comes after three consecutive cash rate hikes this yearFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe Reserve Bank has warned it is ready to raise interest rates further despite leaving its official interest rate on hold at 4.35%.The widely expected decision on Tuesday will bring little relief to mortgage holders, already strained b

stories.headline: Zelenskyy thanks G7 leaders for ‘strong ideas on how to force Russia into peace’ – Europe live
stories.summary: Ukrainian president praises successful talks after Trump’s comments that ‘Russia should make a deal’ after G7 meeting… and given the delay this morning, the meeting may or may not have happened already – guess we will find out at some point during the day.in Évian-les-Bains Continue reading...

stories.headline: India blocks Telegram until Monday due to student exam fraud concerns
stories.summary: A viral youth satirical protest movement, the Cockroach Janta Party, has emerged following exam cancellations last month

stories.headline: Australia warns El Nino weather pattern set to be strongest in decades
stories.summary: Australia’s Bureau of Meteorology says forecasts have pointed towards a 'very strong El Nino event'.

stories.headline: What to know about this week’s US primary elections
stories.summary: Primaries are set to take place in Alabama, California, Oklahoma, Georgia, and the capital, Washington, DC, on Tuesday.

stories.headline: Bangladesh bets $3.2bn to avert a banking crisis but reforms elusive
Germany blocks UniCredit bid for Commerzbank but the share price soars
stories.summary: The federal government, which holds 12% of the shares, has rejected the Italian bank’s bid for the German lender, calling it aggressive and hostile. UniCredit soars on the stock market as the Frankfurt prosecutor investigates alleged market manipulation.

stories.headline: Trump tells Netanyahu 'be responsible with Lebanon, let Syria handle Hezbollah' at G7
stories.summary: Trump questions Netanyahu's methods, says Beirut strike was excessive, and points to Syria’s President as the most “capable” of countering Hezbollah.

stories.headline: Watch: German chancellor surprises Trump with 'Trump 47' football jersey at G7
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz presented U.S. President Donald Trump with a personalized football jersey during the G7 summit in France.

stories.headline: Deadline looms for UniCredit's hostile bid for Commerzbank
stories.summary: Tuesday marks the final day for UniCredit's hostile and highly political takeover offer for Commerzbank, as Italy's second-largest bank has its sights on an ambitious pan-European banking merger.

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

stories.headline: JPMorgan plans Chase expansion into Europe’s largest markets
stories.summary: US lender eyes France, Spain and Italy as it aims to grow digital bank

stories.headline: Binance Prepares for EU Exit With Local License in Limbo
stories.summary: Binance Holdings Ltd. is making preparations to stop providing services in the European Union, as a deadline to receive a license to operate in the bloc draws near and the crypto exchange has yet to receive approval from Greece’s financial regulator.

stories.headline: SpaceX Set to Overtake Microsoft, Amazon in Value as Stock Soars
stories.summary: SpaceX shares jumped on Tuesday, putting the firm on track to overtake both Amazon.com Inc. and Microsoft Corp. to become the fourth largest publicly traded company in the world just days after its blockbuster debut.

stories.headline: SpaceX Pounces on $60 Billion Cursor Takeover Days After IPO
stories.summary: Technically, SpaceX had 30 days following its record-shattering public debut to decide on a $60 billion takeover of the AI coding startup Cursor. In the end, all it took was two trading days.

stories.headline: US Futures Waver as Oil Drops on Iran Hope; SpaceX Extends Gains
stories.summary: US stocks held steady at the open on Tuesday as rising confidence about a US-Iran agreement sent oil prices lower. SpaceX extended gains, as broad market sentiment remained strong.

stories.headline: Marvell Needs Lots of Growth to Achieve Huang’s $1 Trillion Call
stories.summary: Jensen Huang may see Marvell Technology Inc. as the stock market’s “next trillion-dollar company,” but it’s going to take a lot of growth for the chipmaker to even sniff that lofty level.

stories.headline: UzNIF to Start Preparing IPO of Uzbekistan Airways
stories.summary: Uzbekistan’s National Investment Fund, or UzNIF, will start preparations for an initial public offering of Uzbekistan Airways as the fund moves to the next stage of its capital-attraction strategy.

stories.headline: World Cup Exposes Growing Global Rift Over Prediction Markets
stories.summary: This year’s World Cup is the first since prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket exploded to popularity as a new way to bet on sports.

stories.headline: UAE’s Adnoc Hires Oil Veteran to Lead Trading Unit’s Expansion
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co has hired commodities veteran Benoit Roulon to head up its trading arm, part of the long-term expansion plan at the Middle Eastern producer which quit OPEC last month.

stories.headline: Aluminum Holds Loss as China Puts Smelters Into Overdrive
stories.summary: Aluminum steadied near its lowest since March as booming Chinese production boosted supplies and investors weighed the potential return of Middle East smelters to the global market.

stories.headline: Takeaways From JD Vance’s New Book ‘Communion,’ on Faith and His Political Ambitions
stories.summary: In a new memoir, Mr. Vance recalls an “unsettling” meeting with Vatican officials on immigration policy and disavows his infamous disparagement of some Democrats as “childless cat ladies.”

stories.headline: China offers staunch support to Myanmar president during his state visit
stories.summary: The two countries sign 18 cooperation deals, including on free trade and assistance in the event of natural disasters.

stories.headline: Former CAR president on trial in absentia over crimes against humanity
stories.summary: The alleged crimes committed by Bozize's security forces include murder, enforced disappearance, torture and rape.

stories.headline: From oil to batteries, Chinese energy companies flag first-half windfalls
Central banks expect their gold reserves to rise as de-dollarization continues
SK's triple play and Japan's subsea strategy
China retail sales fall for first time since COVID lockdowns
Vietnam's new rich rethink wealth as advisers target a nascent market
Russia's embrace of the Taliban
Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.headline: US and Iran agree deal to end war as Trump says Strait of Hormuz to reopen
stories.summary: Both sides declare "the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon", Pakistan says.

stories.headline: Trump aims to end Iran war but nuclear issue remains unresolved
stories.summary: President Trump says the US and Iran have agreed a deal to end the war but it is unclear what has been agreed on Iran's nuclear programme

stories.headline: Political tension has undermined World Cup joy, says Iran striker
stories.summary: Iran's striker Mehdi Taremi said the country's political predicament "undermines the joy of the World Cup".

stories.headline: Trump sought to break Iran’s regime. He settled for reopening Hormuz.
stories.summary: The emerging agreement ends a costly war but leaves Iran’s leadership intact and its nuclear future still subject to negotiation.

stories.headline: Captain of Russian shadow fleet tanker intercepted in Channel charged
stories.summary: An Indian national is charged with contravening sanctions after the tanker was detained.

stories.headline: Russian strikes kill 11 and set historic cathedral in Kyiv ablaze
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack in the Russian city of Tula, south of Moscow, killed three people.

stories.headline: S&P 500 Rally Wavers Before Fed as SpaceX Jumps: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally that sent stocks to the brink of all-time highs wavered on the eve of the first Federal Reserve rate decision under Kevin Warsh, with a another slide in oil prices driving bond yields lower.

stories.headline: Lawmakers Warn Trump Officials Not to Pursue Arch Project Without Congress
stories.summary: In a letter to Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and national parks officials, several Democrats and a Senate independent said that members of the administration could face fines and even criminal prosecution.

stories.headline: Trump hosts UFC fights on White House lawn
stories.summary: The South Lawn event was meant to celebrate the nation’s 250th anniversary and the president's 80th birthday.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies falling into volcanic crater
stories.summary: Al-Qaqa Ibn Antar had been attempting to climb its walls without safety equipment when he fell in, local authorities say.

stories.headline: South Korea's Starbucks to shut for staff history lesson after backlash
stories.summary: The chain was criticised for a "Tank Day" marketing ploy on the anniversary of a military massacre.

stories.headline: SpaceX Investors at US Brokers Got at Least One IPO Share
stories.summary: Customers at some of the largest retail brokerage firms in the US each received at least a single share in SpaceX’s $86.2 billion IPO, underscoring how the offering was designed to give individual investors a sizable role.

stories.headline: Argentinian activist who spent 50 years looking for disappeared son dies

stories.headline: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike, Trump says
stories.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
stories.summary: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

stories.headline: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
stories.summary: Japanese firms will spend billions on UK infrastructure and offshore wind, Downing Street says.

stories.headline: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
stories.summary: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

stories.headline: Israel carries out air strikes on Lebanon, state media says, as Iran claims deal with US near
stories.summary: The strikes come as Iran's foreign minister says a deal to end fighting with the US is close.

stories.headline: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
stories.summary: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.

stories.headline: Watch: Fans celebrate Mexico's win in World Cup opening match
stories.summary: There were wild celebrations in host nation Mexico, as their team kicked off their World Cup campaign with a 2-0 win over South Africa.

stories.headline: Israeli tech firm accused of targeting First Minister in election
stories.summary: Accounts linked to tech firm BlackCore are alleged to have carried out 'digital interference operations' against John Swinney.

stories.headline: The value of SpaceX rockets on its stock-market debut
stories.summary: Elon Musk has become the world’s first trillionaire

stories.headline: American capitalism is run by millionaires, not billionaires
